summary: M15.2: bit-exact multi-row Marlin-K GEMV lands as infra, but exact weight-amortization of the unsafe W4 verifier sites (single_full_v, single_linear_out) is blocked by argmax fragility, not kernel numerics.

kernel.name: gemv_paro_marlin_k_fma_multi_row_fp16
kernel.property: reads each weight element once, FMAs into all B+1 rows in the same k-order + per-row reduction as single-row Marlin-K
kernel.bit_exact_test: tests/test_qwen35_paro_marlin_k_multi_row.py: rows {2,3,4,5,6,8} x threads {64,128} np.array_equal vs per-row single Marlin-K

finding.decode_multi_row_on_unsafe_sites: flips translation (token ~42) -- earlier probe, also non-exact
finding.root_cause: verifier accumulated-chain input to out_proj/v_proj differs slightly from AR rows==1; argmax in degenerate 248045-repeat regions is knife-edge, so any numerics change tips it. prefill is exact only by coincidence on these prompts.
finding.conclusion: exact weight-amortization of these sites needs verifier input == AR bit-for-bit (chain-state problem), not a better projection kernel. M15.2 blocked; M14.num.1 reframed.

side_discovery.decode_batched_64tok_9prompt: NOT exact 9/9 baseline: translation fails (token-6 reorder + 248045 loop); summarize fragile too. batched is exact 9/9. Extends M14.book.5 (c1_loop drift) to decode_batched.

notes: Kernel is correct + registered + tested; default-off so no behavior change.
Unsafe-site weight-amortization is an input-equivalence problem, not a kernel problem.
